title: DON’T MISS these 12 Autoimmune Warning Signs
claim: Autoimmune diseases are spreading, affecting one in 10 people globally.

title: DON’T MISS these 12 Autoimmune Warning Signs

title: DON’T MISS these 12 Autoimmune Warning Signs

title: DON’T MISS these 12 Autoimmune Warning Signs
claim: Autoimmune diseases frequently overlap, meaning a person might initially present with symptoms of one condition (e.g., dry eyes/mouth in Sjögren's) and later develop signs of another (e.g., joint pain in rheumatoid arthritis or thyroid disease evolving into lupus).

title: DON’T MISS these 12 Autoimmune Warning Signs

title: DON’T MISS these 12 Autoimmune Warning Signs
claim: Lupus can manifest as a distinctive butterfly-shaped rash on the face.

title: DON’T MISS these 12 Autoimmune Warning Signs

title: DON’T MISS these 12 Autoimmune Warning Signs

title: DON’T MISS these 12 Autoimmune Warning Signs

title: DON’T MISS these 12 Autoimmune Warning Signs

title: DON’T MISS these 12 Autoimmune Warning Signs

title: DON’T MISS these 12 Autoimmune Warning Signs

title: DON’T MISS these 12 Autoimmune Warning Signs

title: Why Autism Feels Like Anxiety (But Isn’t)

title: Why Autism Feels Like Anxiety (But Isn’t)
claim: Attempting to reason one's way out of an overloaded nervous system is deeply tiring and largely ineffective because the underlying strain is physiological, not primarily cognitive.

title: Why Autism Feels Like Anxiety (But Isn’t)

title: Why Autism Feels Like Anxiety (But Isn’t)

title: Why Autism Feels Like Anxiety (But Isn’t)

title: Why Autism Feels Like Anxiety (But Isn’t)

title: Why Autism Feels Like Anxiety (But Isn’t)

title: Why Autism Feels Like Anxiety (But Isn’t)
claim: Research suggests that the calming system in many autistic adults does not engage efficiently, causing the body to remain partially activated even in safe situations, making rest and relaxation techniques often ineffective.

title: Why Autism Feels Like Anxiety (But Isn’t)
claim: The persistent bodily activation and ineffective rest experienced by autistic individuals are due to physiological delays, not a failure of mindset or personal effort.

title: Why Autism Feels Like Anxiety (But Isn’t)

title: Why Autism Feels Like Anxiety (But Isn’t)

title: Why Autism Feels Like Anxiety (But Isn’t)
claim: Effective support for autistic individuals involves reducing sensory input (which is regulation, not avoidance), providing structure (which offers stability, not rigidity), allowing early departure (which is pacing, not failure), and offering accommodations for recovery.

title: Why Autism Feels Like Anxiety (But Isn’t)
